records.ety: [F., fr. pardonner to pardon. See Pardon, v. t.]
usage: Used in expressing courteous denial or contradiction; as, I beg your pardon; or in indicating that one has not understood another; as, I beg pardon; or pardon me?.

defs: The act of pardoning; forgiveness, as of an offender, or of an offense; release from penalty; remission of punishment; absolution.
quotes: Pardon, my lord, for me and for my tidings.
But infinite in pardon was my judge.

defs: An official warrant of remission of penalty.
quotes: Sign me a present pardon for my brother.

defs: A release, by a sovereign, or officer having jurisdiction, from the penalties of an offense, being distinguished from amnesty, which is a general obliteration and canceling of a particular line of past offenses.

records.ety: [Either fr. pardon, n., or from F. pardonner, LL. perdonare; L. per through, thoroughly, perfectly + donare to give, to present. See Par-, and Donation.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pardoned (pär"d'nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Pardoning.]

defs: To absolve from the consequences of a fault or the punishment of crime; to free from penalty; -- applied to the offender.
quotes: In this thing the Lord pardon thy servant.
I pray you, pardon me; pray heartily, pardon me.

defs: To remit the penalty of; to suffer to pass without punishment; to forgive; -- applied to offenses.
quotes: I pray thee, pardon my sin.
Apollo, pardon My great profaneness 'gainst thine oracle!

defs: To refrain from exacting as a penalty.
quotes: I pardon thee thy life before thou ask it.
Even now about it! I will pardon you.
